Requirement.Name: Exploration strategy initialization
Requirement.Content: Robot shall initialize exploration state, select an exploration strategy (wall-following or frontier-based), initialize internal map data structures, and begin collecting sensor data to support exploration.

Requirement.Name: Wall detection integration during exploration
Requirement.Content: Robot shall detect walls using available sensors (e.g., LIDAR, sonar) and integrate detections into the occupancy map to avoid collisions while exploring.

Requirement.Name: Real-time collision avoidance during exploration
Requirement.Content: Robot shall perform real-time collision avoidance by stopping, slowing, or re-planning when a potential collision is detected, ensuring a safe navigation margin.

Requirement.Name: Wall detection reliability and integration
Requirement.Content: Robot shall reliably detect walls under varying sensor noise conditions and integrate detections into the occupancy map to support safe exploration.

Requirement.Name: Internal map representation
Requirement.Content: Robot shall implement an internal map data structure (e.g., occupancy grid or graph) with coordinates, resolution, and supports for incremental updates and map queries via a defined API.

Requirement.Name: Cell Classification
Requirement.Content: Robot shall classify cells as free, blocked, or unknown, fuse sensor data to update cell confidence, and maintain consistent occupancy information in the map.

Requirement.Name: Mapping time constraint
Requirement.Content: Mapping shall complete within 5 minutes for a standard maze; progress shall be observable via a status metric; if time is exceeded, the system shall degrade gracefully and continue mapping with best effort.

Requirement.Name: Path planning engine
Requirement.Content: Robot shall compute a path from the current position to the target using algorithms such as A* or D*, considering the current map state and safety constraints, and produce a sequence of waypoints.

Requirement.Name: Path representation and validation
Requirement.Content: Robot shall define a data structure for paths (sequence of poses), validate planned paths against the current map, and ensure path traversability before execution.

Requirement.Name: Dynamic replanning
Requirement.Content: Robot shall monitor for map or sensor changes and trigger replanning within a bounded time, supporting asynchronous updates and partial path handling.

Requirement.Name: Return to start path execution
Requirement.Content: Robot shall compute and execute a route back to the starting position after exploration, verify path safety, and handle interruptions or energy constraints gracefully.

Requirement.Name: Second run execution
Requirement.Content: Robot shall perform a second exploration pass to increase map coverage, applying a potentially different exploration strategy and avoiding previously visited areas where feasible.

Requirement.Name: Efficient path execution for second run
Requirement.Content: Second run shall follow the most efficient path derived from previous planning, apply path smoothing, and adapt execution if obstacles are detected.

Requirement.Name: Collision avoidance during run and exploration
Requirement.Content: Robot shall maintain a safe distance from walls and obstacles, apply emergency stopping if proximity exceeds safety thresholds, and log avoidance events for analysis.

Requirement.Name: Fault tolerance and graceful degradation
Requirement.Content: System shall tolerate sensor or actuator faults by validating inputs, switching to safe modes, using redundant data where available, and continuing operation with degraded performance where possible.

Requirement.Name: Sensor data processing
Requirement.Content: System shall process sensor data according to the sensor data interface description, applying calibration, filtering, time synchronization, and producing standardized measurement sets for mapping and planning.

Requirement.Name: Actuator command interface
Requirement.Content: System shall send control commands to actuators using the motor interface description, including command formatting, timing behavior, acknowledgment handling, and fault reporting.

Requirement.Name: Localization and Pose Estimation
Requirement.Content: Robot shall estimate its pose in the environment using sensor fusion and provide pose with uncertainty; Pose updates shall be integrated into the map and planning modules.
